state.user_prompt: Please implement a user-facing anime storyboard generation website. Users should be able to describe a story idea, generate storyboard frames, view the results, and manage their generation history. The website should provide a large text input area for users to describe their story or scene, along with optional quick prompt chips. For now, the available chips should be: "Anime 2D style", "Warm lighting", and "In the bedroom". The input box should initially contain a default example prompt: "Frame 1: Lulu is resting on the bed. Frame 2: Lulu has a dream and suddenly wakes up from the bed. Frame 3: Lulu then starts crying in fear." It should also include input validation so that empty text cannot be submitted. The page should include a prominent "Generate Storyboard" button. After the user submits, the page should display a clear loading or progress state, along with friendly progress messages that explain in simple language what step the backend is currently working on, so users can easily understand what is happening. It should also prevent duplicate submissions while a task is in progress. Once generation is complete, the page should display the storyboard results in order as a gallery, where each frame card shows the image, frame number, and a short caption or title placeholder, and supports click-to-zoom or lightbox preview. The overall layout should use a responsive grid that works well across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices. Each completed generation should also include a clear summary panel showing the generation ID, total frame count, and generation time or current status. If no result exists yet, the page should display a clear empty-state guidance message. In addition, the website should provide a "Generation History" list so users can revisit previous outputs, open a past generation to view its storyboard frames again, and search or filter the history by generation ID or prompt keywords.

In addition, the website should provide a "Generation History" list so users can revisit previous outputs, open a past generation to view its storyboard frames again, and search or filter the history by generation ID or prompt keywords.
state.research_brief:
REQUEST TYPE
- Classified as: tool
- Research depth used: deep

LIKELY USER TASKS
- search-or-filter interaction
- user-configurable output
- explanatory content flow

INTERACTION SIGNALS
- clear primary action above the fold
- controls and outputs should stay in the same viewport context
- deterministic state feedback for valid and invalid input
- visible result area that changes after interaction
- navigation and section hierarchy that mirrors the main task

CONTENT AND IA SIGNALS
- Organize the page around the primary task implied by: Please implement a user-facing anime storyboard generation website.
Users should be able to:
- describe a story idea
- generate storyboard frames
- view the results
- manage their generation history
The website should provide a large text input area for users to describe their story or scene, along with optional quick prompt chips.
For now, the available chips should be: "Anime 2D style", "Warm lighting", and "In the bedroom".
The input box should initially contain a default example prompt: "Frame 1: Lulu is resting on the bed.
Frame 2: Lulu has a dream and suddenly wakes up from the bed.
Frame 3: Lulu then starts crying in fear." It should also include input validation so that empty text cannot be submitted.
The page should include a prominent "Generate Storyboard" button.
After the user submits, the page should display a clear loading or progress state, along with friendly progress messages that explain in simple language what step the backend is currently working on, so users can easily understand what is happening.
It should also prevent duplicate submissions while a task is in progress.
Once generation is complete, the page should display the storyboard results in order as a gallery, where each frame card shows the image, frame number, and a short caption or title placeholder, and supports click-to-zoom or lightbox preview.
The overall layout should use a responsive grid that works well across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.
Each completed generation should also include a clear summary panel showing the generation ID, total frame count, and generation time or current status.
If no result exists yet, the page should display a clear empty-state guidance message.
In addition, the website should provide a "Generation History" list so users can revisit previous outputs, open a past generation to view its storyboard frames again, and search or filter the history by generation ID or prompt keywords.
- Separate controls, results, and explanatory/supporting sections so the main workflow is obvious.
- If the request implies a tool or dashboard, keep inputs and outputs close together instead of scattering them across decorative sections.

VISUAL AND UX SIGNALS
- Avoid generic white SaaS admin chrome; aim for a premium instrument-panel feel with deliberate contrast.
- Use stronger background atmosphere, sharper dividers, and at least one accent color that breaks the monochrome teal/blue pattern.
- Create a visual hierarchy where KPI areas, control zones, and narrative sections have distinct surface treatments.

IMPLEMENTATION SIGNALS
- Prefer browser-only interactive behavior with deterministic mock data when real services are not available.
- Planner should convert implied capabilities into explicit DOM ids, actions, result targets, and acceptance checks.
- Generator should implement real JS state changes, not static mockup copy.

ACCEPTANCE SIGNALS
- presence of explicit input controls and submit actions
- visible loading, success, and error states
- at least three result fields update for the core task

SOURCE NOTES
- No reliable external sources were captured.

sections.title: Primary navigation
sections.purpose: Sticky navigation with logo, branding, and section jump links.

sections.title: Hero
sections.purpose: Above-the-fold introduction and primary value proposition for the Anime Storyboard Generator.

sections.title: Storyboard Creator Workspace
sections.purpose: Main input area containing the large story text area, quick prompt chips ('Anime 2D style', 'Warm lighting', 'In the bedroom'), input validation, and the primary 'Generate Storyboard' button.

sections.title: Active Generation Results
sections.purpose: Displays friendly loading/progress states, the generation summary panel (ID, frame count, time), empty-state guidance, and the responsive image gallery of generated frames with click-to-zoom/lightbox features.

sections.title: Generation History
sections.purpose: Provides a list of past generations that users can revisit, complete with a search and filter bar for finding specific generation IDs or prompt keywords.

sections.title: Footer
sections.purpose: Closing branding, helpful resources, and standard footer links.

page_outline.notes: Ensuring a premium instrument-panel UI by placing the creator inputs and active results adjacent to each other for immediate deterministic feedback, with clear visual hierarchy for the history dashboard.

state.design_spec:
Use a robust CSS Grid for the 'generation-results' section to allow smooth reflow of 1 to N frames from mobile up to desktop widths.
Implement a dedicated JS state machine to manage 'idle', 'generating', 'success', and 'error' states, physically swapping DOM visibility between the empty-state message and the results gallery.
Initialize the main textarea with the requested default Lulu story prompt via HTML value attribute.
Ensure 'Generate' button disables visually and functionally (via 'disabled' attribute) during active processing to prevent duplicate submissions.
Style the prompt chips as interactive badges that elegantly append their text to the main input box when clicked, updating the input's value dynamically.
Utilize a fixed or absolute positioned DIV for the lightbox overlay that listens for clicks outside the image to dismiss.

visual_direction.creative_rationale: Creates a focused, premium workspace feeling reminiscent of professional film editing or animation studio software, using dark tones to make colorful anime frames pop.
visual_direction.layout_motif: Dashboard-like adjacencies with floating panels, clear input-output segregation, and edge-to-edge frame galleries.
visual_direction.surface_treatment: Deep matte backgrounds with tinted semi-transparent foreground cards (glassmorphism), accented by sharp 1px teal borders.
visual_direction.background_treatment: Very dark navy backdrop with a subtle radial teal glow originating from behind the primary workspace.
visual_direction.shape_language: Sharply rounded corners (4px to 8px), structured grid cells, and clean horizontal dividers.
visual_direction.motion_mood: Snappy, deterministic, with staggered fade-ins for generated frames.
avoid: overly playful or cartoonish UI borders

inferred_features.label: Storyboard Generation Engine
inferred_features.goal: Submit story text and parameters to receive sequenced anime frames

inferred_features.label: Quick Prompt Appender
inferred_features.goal: Allow users to quickly add stylistic tags to their prompt

inferred_features.label: History Filter
inferred_features.goal: Find past generations by keyword or ID

inferred_features.label: Frame Lightbox Preview
inferred_features.goal: View a generated storyboard frame in full resolution

updates: show generation-status-panel with rotating progress messages (e.g., 'Analyzing story beats...', 'Rendering anime styles...', 'Finalizing frames...')
populate results-gallery with mock images after delay
core_user_actions.data_source: in-page mock dataset of generated frames

updates: append chip text to prompt-input value

validation_hints: if prompt-input is empty on submit, show input-error-msg and halt
generate-btn must be disabled during the simulated generation delay to prevent duplicate submissions
lightbox overlay must close on Escape key or outside click
acceptance_checks: Submitting an empty textarea shows a validation error.
Clicking 'Anime 2D style' chip adds the text into the textarea.
Clicking Generate transitions UI to a loading state with friendly text updates, then displays 3 frame cards and updates the summary panel.
Clicking on a generated frame opens it in a full-screen lightbox.
Typing in the history search bar instantly filters the visible past generation cards.
